transcript:
Hey everyone. How's it going? So, here today to talk to you guys about our latest work, um, tokenizing predictions. Uh, earlier this month, we released the DFlow Prediction Markets API, which is powered by Kalshi. And, uh, I want to tell everyone here a little bit more about how it's built, what to expect from it, and what's coming up in 2026.

Before we get into that though, let me talk a little bit about the background. What DFlow actually does, what we're building, and what we spent 2025 doing. So, DFlow builds low-latency trading infrastructure. What this means concretely today is we operate a Solana DEX aggregator. We aggregate 100% of liquidity on the network, construct a very large graph of prices, and then solve a very difficult optimization problem in order to determine the best routes from a token a user wants to give up for a token that they want to receive.

This year has been pretty phenomenal for our growth. Um, we have facilitated over 33 billion in trading volume since April 2025. We've contributed a lot to the app revenue that applications on the network have, uh, received. Um, month-over-month growth has been very strong and we've serviced over a million users across the globe.

In January of 2025, we released our DFlow Spot Trading API. This is our Solana DEX aggregator, uh, which we aggregated the spot tokens on the network from, and released this API so that applications and wallets could access the liquidity from the network. It took us about six months and, uh, in June of 2025 we had four applications integrate our Spot Trading API. At this point in time, we had about 4 billion in cumulative volume that we had processed for the network through our contract, uh, and about 2 million in revenue paid out to applications.

In June of 2025, we also released sort of our new backend. This allowed streamlined DEX integrations. It was a quiet release, but it let us integrate DEXes very quickly. By September of 2025, we had 26 applications integrate the DFlow aggregator. Uh, we processed about 10 billion in cumulative volume and we had paid 16 million in app revenue out.

In September of 2025, we also released what I think is Solana's most exciting microstructure improvement of 2025, Just-In-Time Routing. Just-In-Time Routing was a zero-to-one mechanism that was released on the network at this time. Just-In-Time Routing let a transaction which was previously frozen become dynamic on-chain at the time that it was processed. This led to drastically better execution for users, a lot of saved, uh, uh, price slippage that was returned to users' wallets or that could be captured by applications in application revenue.

By October of 2025, we had processed 18 billion in cumulative volume and we had paid out 24 million in application revenue. Very exciting growth, and at this point in time we had also expanded the team quite a bit.

In October of 2025, we released a new Prop AMM integration backend. We spent a lot of time thinking about microstructure on the network. Prop AMMs, as you heard from Ben before this, have been a very integral part of the process of providing great execution on-chain. Spreads that are tighter indeed than Binance. This integration, this new backend, allowed Prop AMMs to integrate super smoothly into DFlow's routing, uh, accessing features that were not available anywhere else, such as Just-In-Time Routing.

By November of 2025, last month, we had over 75 applications integrate, with 33 billion in cumulative trading volume and over 34 million paid out in application revenue. Super exciting.

Earlier this month, in fact a few days ago, we released the DFlow Prediction Markets API powered by Kalshi. So what exac— what exactly is this? Kalshi, as you guys know, is a prediction market, uh, operator. They operate prediction markets in sort of an off-chain regulated way. Um, you know, and and the Solana network is sort of where some of the world's most opinionated and active traders live. DFlow is the connective tissue, and in particular our Prediction Markets API is the connective tissue between traders on the Solana network and the liquidity that lives on Kalshi.

So, what does this actually mean for users? I think starting this month, and uh, there will be a pretty exciting announcement later today, you guys should uh, keep your eyes peeled for. Um, Solana users will begin to receive market access to one of the most liquid prediction markets exchanges in the world. In exchange, Kalshi receives access to some of the most opinionated, active degens that the world has ever seen.

But how do we do this? We we tokenize Kalshi, but how does this actually work under the hood? So under the hood, uh, we have released what we're calling a Concurrent Liquidity Program. A Concurrent Liquidity Program is something that will actually glue liquidity from off-chain sources like Kalshi to the network in a way that when a user actually trades with, um, a Concurrent Liquidity Program, they're being receiv— they're receiving a a token, an SPL token that actually represents their position. In the case of a prediction market, they can redeem these tokens for stablecoins.

The way this is done is in a multi-transaction flow. The first transaction that actually opens the order, increases the user's position, uh, escrows some of their funds. The second transaction, uh, facilitates the trade, actually performs the fill, mints them the token representing their position. Similar multi-transaction flow when it comes to redeeming the stablecoins. The position is burnt in the prediction market case, and the user receives stablecoins if their prediction was correct.

So, why is tokenization important? Tokenization was a choice. In building this Prediction Markets API, we did not need to tokenize. Um, but we felt it was really important to do. And we didn't have sort of like a clear sense of why exactly it was important to do, but we understood the serendipitous nature of tokens and DeFi. The fact that you can compose liquidity, compose tokens, and have novel mechanisms designed by anyone was just phenomenal.

So we took a bet on serendipity, and uh, this is also a a bet on the imagination of builders on Solana, and and we tokenized uh Kalshi's prediction markets. So some of the ways in which I expect this sort of serendipitous nature of tokenization to take form is in perhaps borrowing lending protocols, in sort of new liquidity formation mechanisms. You know, we've seen uh some some really cool stuff come out of nowhere uh in the past, such as pump. Um, and we've also seen sort of like more serious but also very exciting things like Prop AMMs come. We are very excited to see what builders with on Solana do with uh prediction tokens.

The last sort of thing category here is a party emoji. We have no idea what is coming next, but we're very excited for it. Not only are we excited for it, builders are very excited for it. So, to date, we have over 100 integrations lined up. Most of which will be going live in the next quarter. A lot of which will be going live this month as well. Some of which will be going live, or at least being announced today.

From the builders that we've been talking to, we've seen some really exciting applications come up. Social trading has been a common theme. Applications that address sort of niche markets within sort of prediction markets which are very general. Data vendors have reached out to us. Group trading applications. Uh, I think a lot of people here interact with each other in Telegram, and there's some really novel things you can do with prediction markets that are on-chain and then accessible through things like Telegram. We've also seen uh interest in yield products.

So, what can you do today? You can go and look at the Prediction Markets API. Uh, you can get started. Um, and then reach out to us and we will help you build whatever you can imagine. Thank you.

# Kalshi Launches Tokenized Prediction Markets on Solana Through DFlow Integration
CFTC-regulated exchange brings event contracts onchain with $2 million builder grants program
,and[Blockhead](https://www.blockhead.co/author/blockhead/)
December 2, 2025. 2:14 PM
2 min read
* [𝕏]()
* [Share on Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.blockhead.co/2025/12/02/kalshi-launches-tokenized-prediction-markets-on-solana-through-dflow-integration/)
* [Share on Pinterest]()
* [Share on LinkedIn]()
* [Share on WhatsApp]()
* [Share via Email]()
![]()
## Table of Contents
CFTC-regulated prediction market exchange Kalshi has launched tokenized versions of its markets on Solana through an[integration](https://solana.com/news/dflow-prediction-markets-api?ref=blockhead.co)with DFlow and Jupiter, bringing federally regulated event contracts to blockchain infrastructure for the first time.
The DFlow Prediction Markets API enables developers to access all Kalshi markets as SPL tokens on Solana, allowing prediction market positions to be traded, borrowed, lent, or used as collateral within decentralized finance protocols. Kalshi is supporting the ecosystem with a $2 million grants program for builders.
> Tokenized predictions powered by Kalshi are live on [> @Solana
](https://twitter.com/solana?ref_src=twsrc^tfw&ref=blockhead.co)
> $2M+ in builder grants are open, [> @AxiomExchange
](https://twitter.com/AxiomExchange?ref_src=twsrc^tfw&ref=blockhead.co)> is next, and more chains coming soon.
> Welcome to the "Powered by Kalshi" era. [> pic.twitter.com/0zlNoyZNDA
](https://t.co/0zlNoyZNDA?ref=blockhead.co)
> —Kalshi (@Kalshi) [> December 1, 2025
](https://twitter.com/Kalshi/status/1995585557930672473?ref_src=twsrc^tfw&ref=blockhead.co)
The move represents a significant expansion for prediction markets into decentralized finance infrastructure. While platforms like Polymarket have operated on blockchain rails, Kalshi's integration marks the first time a CFTC-regulated exchange has offered tokenized market access with full composability across DeFi protocols.
Jupiter, Solana's largest decentralized exchange aggregator, will route trades through the tokenized markets alongside its existing liquidity sources, potentially exposing millions of users to prediction market trading.
"Tokenization is the endgame. It is non-custodial, instant, and crypto native," Kalshi said in announcing the launch. The integration uses a hybrid request-for-quote system that executes trades onchain while drawing liquidity from Kalshi's regulated exchange.
Founded in 2018, Kalshi became the first CFTC-designated contract market for event-based derivatives in 2020, establishing a regulated framework for trading on outcomes ranging from economic data to political events. The exchange has since built what it describes as the deepest prediction market liquidity globally.
The tokenization layer enables Kalshi's market coverage to be accessed programmatically on Solana. When users place orders through the DFlow API, liquidity providers fill those orders and the protocol mints tokens representing the prediction position. When markets resolve, winning tokens can be redeemed for stablecoins.
DFlow's integration uses Concurrent Liquidity Programs, a Solana-native framework that bridges offcha

Kalshi, a regulated prediction market platform, has rolled out tokenized versions of its event contracts on the Solana blockchain, aiming to bridge traditional finance with the world of decentralized finance.
## Key Takeaways
* Kalshi has launched tokenized prediction markets on Solana, enabling onchain trading of event contracts such as US elections, economic data, and sports outcomes.
* Integration with DeFi protocols Jupiter and DFlow allows users to trade, lend, borrow, or use prediction positions as collateral within the Solana ecosystem.
* Kalshi is supporting the initiative with a $2 mill

[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f0866da8eed4c/6867a1f64ca2c75f0d14f371_16c97ce9017b907297827c5113a068f9_Frame%201597880158.avif)](/)

## Introducing BET: The First Capital-Efficient Prediction Market on Solana
August 19, 2024
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f08e5798eed50/66c1d9da828d359cdf3d425e_Blog%20banner.png)
[
Contents
](#)
[
Contents
](#)
[
](https://twitter.com/driftprotocol)[
](https://discord.com/invite/fMcZBH8ErM)
[
Trade Now
](https://app.drift.trade/)
![]()
![]()
B.E.T launches against the backdrop of the most exciting US election season yet, with the first prediction markets centered around the US elections. Keep your eyes peeled for more market categories to come across sports (F1, CryptoFightNight), crypto and culture (Solana debate outcomes).
Looking ahead, Drift plans to elevate the prediction market user experience while it builds towards fully permissionless markets through governance later this year.
## Why be (B)ullish on (E)very(T)hing on Drift?
B.E.T is built on top of Drift Protocol, making it the most capital efficient prediction market product out there. This means you benefit from its borrow/lend platform, cross-collateral capabilities, and derivatives.
Here's how:
* **Earn yield on all your B.E.T trades:**Each trade earns yield automatically, powered by Drift’s borrow/lend engine.
* **Hedge on a Unified Platform:**B.E.T allows the creation of “structured bets” —Create hedged positions by going long on a prediction market while simultaneously shorting Bitcoin. Do it all on Drift.
* **Utilize over 30+ tokens:**Use your favorite Solana tokens —not just USDC. You can use 30+ tokens from yield-generating stables to LSTs!
Fun fact: B.E.T stands for Bullish on Everything.
## How does B.E.T work?
1. Predict the future outcome of events by buying YES or NO shares.
2. Place a market order to get in right away or a limit order to get in at your desired price.
3. Watch your trade automatically earn yield before the market resolves.
4. The market resolves at the stated date.
You don’t need to wait for the market to resolve, you can sell your shares at any time. For more in depth details, read[here](https://docs.drift.trade/prediction-markets/prediction-markets-intro).
## FUEL up your BETs
B.E.T trades will have a 5x FUEL boost for 2 weeks. FUEL is Drift’s rewards program.
**With FUEL boost:**For every $1 trade placed, you will receive 5 FUEL.
‍**Without FUEL boost:**For every $1 trade placed (either a YES or NO share), you will receive 1 FUEL.
**Want even more FUEL?**
In addition to your trading activity, you can earn 10 FUEL for every DRIFT staked! As Drift DAO works to move towards offering permissionless markets, DRIFT stakers will play a key role in determining future markets. Stay tuned for more updates as this governance model takes shape.
FUEL will be available for redemption of prizes across the Drift and Solana ecosystem at a later date.
—**Note:**B.E.T is available in all countries where Drift can be accessed.

tags.impact_reason: DFlow's prediction market API and existing DEX aggregator are solid updates to Solana's DeFi infrastructure, but not ecosystem-shattering.

The loom metaphor captures the intricate routing and aggregation DFlow performs. For the integrated text, I chose 'YES / NO' displayed on a holographic HUD. This is the fundamental binary outcome of the Kalshi prediction markets being tokenized, making it a perfect diegetic representation of the underlying asset.

editorial.headline: DFlow Tokenizes Regulated Kalshi Markets for Solana DeFi
editorial.standfirst: The integration bridges CFTC-regulated event contracts with Solana's high-velocity trading environment, unlocking a new asset class for on-chain composability. By wrapping off-chain liquidity into SPL tokens, developers can now construct yield, lending, and social trading instruments atop real-world outcomes.
key_takeaways: DFlow and Kalshi launch the Prediction Markets API, utilizing a "Concurrent Liquidity Program" to mint SPL tokens backed by regulated off-chain positions.
Since April 2025, DFlow has processed over $33 billion in cumulative volume and distributed $34 million in revenue to integrating applications.
Over 100 integrations are queued for deployment through Q1 2026, focusing on social trading, data vending, and yield-bearing prediction products.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The convergence of regulated derivatives and decentralized finance has arrived on **Solana**, driven by a new infrastructure layer that transforms binary event contracts into composable assets. **DFlow** has officially deployed its Prediction Markets API, powered by **Kalshi**, effectively turning the CFTC-regulated exchange's liquidity into tradeable SPL tokens. This move represents a significant evolution in market structure: rather than simply routing trades, **DFlow** is acting as the "connective tissue" between institutional-grade off-chain markets and the high-frequency speculative environment of Solana's on-chain economy.

Technically, this integration relies on a novel mechanism termed the **Concurrent Liquidity Program**. Unlike traditional bridges that often introduce latency or security fragmentation, this system glues off-chain liquidity to the network via a multi-transaction flow. When a user engages with the API, the protocol escrows funds and instantaneously mints an SPL token representing the specific prediction position. This tokenization is the critical unlock; it transforms a static bet on **Kalshi** into a liquid asset that can be utilized across the DeFi stack—potentially as collateral in lending protocols or as a base asset for structured yield products.

The strategic timing of this release aligns with **DFlow's** massive scaling in 2025. having processed over $33 billion in volume since April and refining execution through **Just-In-Time Routing**. By leveraging this established low-latency infrastructure, the protocol is betting on "serendipity"—the idea that providing builders with tokenized access to real-world outcomes will spawn entirely new application categories. With over 100 integrations already in the pipeline for Q1 2026, the market is likely to see a surge of niche interfaces, from Telegram-based trading bots to complex hedging instruments, all deriving value from regulated prediction markets.
